arts.sum: International football is consigned to the memory bank for 2024 and there is a full slate of Championship action this weekend as Phil Agius previews all 12 games.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough substitutes dilemma and 'massive strength' verdict for crucial moments
arts.sum: Michael Carrick has offered insight into his thinking when it comes to making substitutions in games - an aspect of his management at Middlesbrough that has at times come under scrutiny. “It’s never just about the 11 players on the pitch at the start of the game," Carrick said.

arts.ttl: ‘A tough place to go’ – Oxford United vs Middlesbrough prediction: The72
arts.sum: The72's writers offer their Oxford United vs Middlesbrough prediction ahead of the Championship clash on Saturday. The post ‘A tough place to go’ – Oxford United vs Middlesbrough prediction: The72 appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: One tough Middlesbrough selection dilemma at Oxford with midfield injuries also a factor
arts.sum: With players returning from international duty and some injuries to contend with, Carrick will have some selection dilemmas for Oxford - with the fact it's the start of a three-game week no doubt a consideration too. With plenty of attacking options in his squad, Carrick could also consider dropping Riley McGree or Finn Azaz into the deeper role - with both having previously played as central midfielders.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough's January transfer planning and Carrick's hint on potential squad manoeuvring
arts.sum: Boro are likely to have a relaxed stance to January's window - happy to monitor what options might crop up, without desperately chasing something, with no real major gaps in their squad as things stand that urgently need addressing. Middlesbrough are starting to plan for the January transfer window, with Michael Carrick offering hints that there could still be room to manoeuvre in their squad in order to create gaps for strengthening.

arts.ttl: Rav van den Berg and Lukas Engel near injury returns as Middlesbrough's plans are revealed
arts.sum: Rav van den Berg and Lukas Engel are both nearing returns for Middlesbrough after spells on the sidelines through injury, with both able to do some light training over the last week or so. Explaining Boro's plans for easing the pair back into the fold, Carrick said: “Both Lukas and Rav are back on the grass.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough suffer Aidan Morris injury blow while Hayden Hackney is assessed ahead of Oxford
arts.sum: However, travelling back to the United States last week for international duty, the 23-year-old was forced to miss both games for his country after suffering a knee injury that is going to keep him out for a good few weeks. Aidan Morris is facing a number of weeks out with a knee injury suffered while in training with the USA, in what is a big blow for Middlesbrough after his excellent start at the club.
